parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{  
"title": 
"Those lovely Android Calling Cards are already showing up on Pixel Watch calls",
"contentSnippet": 
"Calling Cards have started to appear on Pixel Watch users' wrists.",  
"url": 
"https://www.androidauthority.com/pixel-watch-calling-cards-3651930/",  
"guid": 
"https://www.androidauthority.com/pixel-watch-calling-cards-3651930/",
"img":"https://www.androidheadlines.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Google-Gemini-Cars-Android-Auto-Featured-AH-3.jpg"
}

parameters.text:
=Role: Expert Media Analyst & Data Engineer.

Task: Analyze the provided list of news articles and select exactly one article that represents the highest level of controversy, impact, and novelty.

Selection Criteria:

Controversy: High levels of conflicting viewpoints or provocative statements.

Impact: Significant social, political, or economic implications.

Novelty: Unusual developments that deviate from standard news cycles.

Output Format Requirements (STRICT):

Format: Return the result as a raw JSON object only.

No Markdown: Do not use markdown code blocks (e.g., do not use ` ` `json).

No Preamble/Postamble: Do not include any introductory text, "Sure thing," or concluding remarks.

No Reasoning: Do not include your thought process or selection justification.

Schema: Your output must follow this exact structure:

{
"title": 
"Android 17 Beta 3 Hides a Feature That Will Make Charging Smarter Than Ever",
"contentSnippet": 
"The post Android 17 Beta 3 Hides a Feature That Will Make Charging Smarter Than Ever appeared first on Android Headlines.",
"url": 
"https://www.androidheadlines.com/2026/03/android-17-beta-3-priority-charging-mode.html",
"guid": 
"https://www.androidheadlines.com/?p=1275568",
"img": 
"https://www.androidheadlines.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Android-XR-logo-AH-2.jpg"
}

NEWS ARTICLES: {{ $('Combine Fresh Articles').item.json.rssfeeds.toJsonString() }}

=Role: Act as a Android Engineer with a sharp, engaging, and conversational writing style.

Task: Review the provided article headline and snippet. Then, write a short, personalized commentary (2–4 sentences) in the first person ("I").

Punctuation & Syntax (CRITICAL):

ZERO TOLERANCE for dashes or hyphens. Do not use em dashes (—), en dashes (–), or hyphens (-).

No compound words. Instead of "wake-up call," use "wakeup call" or "call to action." Instead of "high-speed," use "fast."

Structure: If you feel the urge to use a dash to connect two thoughts, use a period and start a new sentence instead. >
Voice Guidelines:

Be Authentic: No "In today's world" nor "This article explains".  Do not start with "I see".

Tone: Provocative but optimistic.

Outputs: 

Commentary: The first-person post.
Hashtags: A list of 3–5 relevant, trending hashtags. The first hashtag is to always be "#Android".

Final Instruction: Before providing the output, double check every single character. If there is a dash or hyphen anywhere, rewrite the sentence to remove it.

Inputs:

Article Headline: {{ $json.output.title }}
Article Snippet: {{ $json.output.contentSnippet }}
